title: Slovakian Meat Stew
description: A hearty, slow-cooked stew originating from Slovakia, typically served with a side of bread or dumplings. This recipe combines the rich flavors of meat and vegetables in a savory broth.

ingredients.name: beef chuck, cut into 2-inch pieces
ingredients.notes: Choose a cut with good marbling for tenderness

ingredients.name: onion, chopped
ingredients.notes: Use a sweet onion like Vidalia or Maui for added flavor

ingredients.name: carrots, peeled and chopped
ingredients.notes: Use a mix of orange and yellow carrots for added color

ingredients.name: potatoes, peeled and chopped
ingredients.notes: Choose high-starch potatoes like Russet or Idaho

ingredients.name: garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Use fresh garlic for the best flavor

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: Use a light dusting of flour to thicken the stew

ingredients.name: beef broth
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality broth for added depth of flavor

ingredients.name: red wine
ingredients.notes: Use a dry red wine like Cabernet Sauvignon or Merlot

instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the beef and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the beef from the pot and set aside.
Add the onion, carrots, and potatoes to the pot and cook until the vegetables are slightly tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the beef broth and red wine to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the beef to the pot and bring the stew to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2-3 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh parsley or thyme.
